notes: Refreshed in spec 39 (pr-comments hardening v2). The W011 severity rose from medium to high between baseline capture and this rerun against the v1.41 SKILL.md; the underlying mitigations (untrusted_comment_body framing, 64 KB body size guard, diff-context validation gate, screening-independence note, argument validators) are documented in the new ## Security model section. Snyk's W011 fires on the presence of `gh api .../comments` ingestion patterns regardless of mitigations — the substantive default-mode defense is the Step 6 diff-context validation gate (path/line check plus diff-hunk content check) backed by auto-mode escalation to a confirmation prompt on any screening-flagged or oversized comment, or any Step 6 diff-validation downgrade (the path/line `decline`, the diff-hunk-drift `decline`, and the missing-data `fix` downgrade); `--manual` mode additionally restores the Step 7 confirmation gate before any edit, commit, or push. v1.42 refresh on spec-40 PR: documentation-only update to the Step 1 Arguments prose to describe the `.strip(" \t")` + `\Z` validation contract already enforced by `tests/pr-comments/conftest.py` since the M-iteration (no validation-surface change). Scanner not re-run locally on 2026-05-17 — SNYK_TOKEN unavailable; baseline pin (W011, high) is expected to remain stable because the ingestion-pattern heuristic is unchanged.
